input.prompt:
I'm starting a new console app on .NET 11 and I need to serialize this record to JSON:

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,
not `name`/`age` or `firstName` style). I want to use whatever the framework
provides out of the box rather than writing my own naming-policy class.

Please show me the full program targeting `net11.0` and run it so I can see the
JSON output.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new console app on .NET 11 and I need to serialize this record to JSON:

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,
not `name`/`age` or `firstName` style). I want to use whatever the framework
provides out of the box rather than writing my own naming-policy class.

Please show me the full program targeting `net11.0` and run it so I can see the
JSON output.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET 11 console app that serializes a `Person` record to JSON with PascalCase property names using built-in framework features. In .NET 11 (which would be .NET 9's successor - actually .NET 11 would be released in 2026), System.Text.Json should have a built-in `PascalCase` naming policy.

Let me think about this. .NET versioning: .NET 5 (2020), .NET 6 (2021), .NET 7 (2022), .NET 8 (2023), .NET 9 (2024), .NET 10 (2025), .NET 11 (2026).


System.Text.Json has built-in naming policies like `CamelCase`, `SnakeCaseLower`, `SnakeCaseUpper`, `KebabCaseLower`, and `KebabCaseUpper` (added in .NET 8), but no `PascalCase` policy since the default already preserves C# property names as-is. However, with a positional record like `Person(string name, int age)`, the compiler generates properties with lowercase names matching the parameters, so I'd need to handle the casing conversion. I'm checking what naming policies are actually available in .NET 8 and later versions—there's `CamelCase`, `SnakeCase`, and `KebabCase` variants, but no built-in `PascalCase`. Since we're targeting .NET 11, I should just create the project and see what's available rather than guessing.

data.content: Good — .NET 11 preview SDK is available. Let me create the project and check if `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` exists.
